svg.text: Server-side express-session. SESSION_SECRET is stored as an environment secret — never in code. The
browser stores only a session cookie, no sensitive data.
Global: 100 requests per 15 min per IP. Auth endpoint: 10 login attempts per 15 min. Feedback POST:
20 submissions per 10 min. Prevents brute-force and flooding.
Zod schema validation on every API route — type checks, value ranges, and required fields. Server
returns field-level error messages; invalid input never reaches the database.
Student login ID is never written to the feedback table. Only the system-generated Reference ID is
stored. Even a database administrator cannot trace feedback to a student.
Server validates: course belongs to student's department, faculty is assigned to that course.
Cross-department submissions are rejected at the server, independent of client-side checks.
Feedback window is verified server-side on every submission. Client-side bypass has no effect — the
server checks the database for an active window before accepting any record.
All database access goes through Drizzle ORM with parameterized queries. SQL injection is
effectively impossible — no string interpolation in queries.
Server errors are logged with Pino (JSON format). Clients receive generic error messages — stack
traces and database details are never exposed in API responses.
8.4. Anonymity Architecture -- How It Is Guaranteed
When a student submits feedback, the server generates a cryptographically unique Reference ID (format:
FB-YYYYMMDD-XXXXXX). This ID is stored in the feedback record. The student's login ID (e.g. CUPGS/CSE/042) is
NEVER written to the feedback table. There is no column, no join, and no log entry that links a student's identity
reference_id, course_id, dept_id, semester, year, ratings, comments, custom_answers
NEVER STORED in the feedback table:
student_id, employee_id, name, login credentials, IP address, browser fingerprint
- Even the developer and database admin cannot trace feedback back to a specific student.
- Students can verify their own submission using their Reference ID — but no one else can use it to identify them.
Validation errors (HTTP 400): Field-specific error messages from Zod schema; user sees
Auth errors (HTTP 401): Session missing or expired — client is redirected to the login
Rate limit exceeded (HTTP 429): Includes Retry-After header; client shows a countdown
Server errors (HTTP 500): Logged with Pino for developer review; client receives a safe
